what_it_does: Renders a 'Fleet' panel that displays the live model registry as a navigable surface, grouping models by kind ('chat' and 'embed'), showing counts, a refresh control, fail-loud endpoint errors, and a visual dot whose meaning is explicitly limited to registry membership.
observations: Uses shared kit components (SectionHead, LaneHead, Badge, Surface, EmptyState) for all visual elements.
Maps kinds to kit tones via KIND_TONE: chat='sig', embed='wire', fallback='dim'.
FleetKind renders a lane header with a count chip, a fail-tone Surface for errors, an EmptyState when no models exist, or a list of non-clickable Surface cards per model.
Each model card shows a two-letter monogram, the model name, and a dot whose tooltip/legend states it means 'listed in the live registry'.
Fleet computes a total live model count, shows a loading badge until loaded, and provides a refresh button calling refreshFleet.
The root element carries data-ui-ref='ui://models' for addressability.
Comments extensively document design rationale: source-of-truth from registry, honest 'alive' semantics, fail-loud errors, and conversion to the shared kit language.
standouts: Heavy inline documentation framing the UI as an 'honest' signal rather than a fabricated health indicator.
Fail-loud error presentation using the same kit Surface tone='fail' pattern as other regions.
conventions: React functional components with TypeScript props.
Imports shared components from '../components/kit' and state from '../AppContext'.
Class-name strings built by concatenation for layout-only local styling.
concerns: Only 'chat' and 'embed' kinds are hardcoded; other registry kinds would require code changes.
The monogram is always name.slice(0,2).toUpperCase(), which can look odd for very short or non-alphabetic names.
Total live count is recomputed inline on every render with duplicated logic.
The refresh button has no visible label and relies on a title attribute and symbol, which may be less accessible.
err is typed as string but the component treats it as truthy/falsy without distinguishing empty string.
notes: A design-rationale-heavy React panel that renders the live model registry using shared kit components and refuses to fake per-model health status.
questions: What is the exact shape of the fleet object returned by useApp?
How does refreshFleet re-fetch data, and does it handle loading/error states?
What other kinds can the registry return, and how would they be added?
Where is the data-ui-ref='ui://models' routing/address resolution implemented?
What are the full prop interfaces and tone options for the kit components?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to make the live model registry visible as an honest, navigable panel, and the code matches that purpose closely.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn the invisible model registry into a trustworthy, commander-bridge-style panel that tells the user exactly what the registry knows and no more, while aligning visually with the rest of the application through the shared kit.
novelty: The explicit 'honest alive' semantics: a model dot means registry membership, not a heartbeat, and this meaning is repeated in tooltip, legend, and comments to prevent misreading.
contribution: A registry-backed, refreshable fleet panel that surfaces live model names per kind, exposes endpoint failures loudly, uses shared kit components consistently, and provides an addressable DOM anchor for the model surface.
summary_for_embedding: Fleet.tsx is a React region component that renders an addressable live model fleet panel, grouping chat and embed models from a registry-backed context with refresh, count badges, fail-loud errors, and a visual dot legend indicating registry membership.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to make the live model registry visible on the canvas as an honest, navigable, design-system-aligned surface without fabricating per-model health status.

symbols.does: Renders one kind's group header, error card, empty state, or list of model cards.
symbols.description: FleetKind is a helper component that receives a kind label, an array of model names, and an optional error string. It chooses a kit tone for the kind, renders a LaneHead with a count chip, and either a fail-tone Surface for the error, an EmptyState when no models are present, or a series of non-clickable Surface cards—each showing a two-letter monogram, the model name, and an honest live-registry dot.

symbols.does: Renders the full Fleet panel with header, refresh control, kind groups, and legend.
symbols.description: Fleet is the exported region component. It pulls fleet state and refreshFleet from useApp, computes a total live model count, renders a SectionHead with a live-count or loading badge plus a refresh button, renders FleetKind groups for chat and embed models, and adds a visible legend explaining the dot's registry-membership meaning. The root element carries data-ui-ref='ui://models'.
